item.title: handmade Motoko cosplay (Ghost In The Shell)
item.description:
A project I've been working on since May! I always loved GITS and the whole Cyberpunk genre as a whole so I've been wanting to do this for the longest time!
And since I'm quite the perfectionist when it comes to my craft, throughout this whole process my goal was to stay true to Motoko's design. I decided to go for latex fabric - even though I knew it would be quite the challenge to make a structured corset as my first attempt at working with it :D Took SO many mock-ups until I finally achieved the desired shape which honestly was expected but latex is such an expensive fabric and... it kinda hurt when I has to redo things haha
item.dc:creator: annahimejima

item.title: Happy 4th and happy goal weight day!
item.description:
Left is 194.7#, back in December... and right is today, at 145!
Those grey shorts used to be tight in me, now they barely stay up. Better than that, I feel better, I sleep better, and my mental health is better. Shout out to my doc for the help, my dogs for the love, and ME for getting it done!
Puppy tax Elli Bay
Puppy tax Red. Happy 4th to all my fellow Americans fighting to good fight how we can!
item.dc:creator: MadamPuddifoot

item.title: The SAVE act is dead!
item.description: https://x.com/DemzDeliver/status/2073119198637686984 https://thehill.com/homenews/senate/5951419-thom-tillis-senate-gop-save-america-act-fate/ TLDR - Dems killed the SAVE act, Trump's (currently) biggest grab to rig the upcoming elections. Now there is no time to implement new voting rules before NOV. Hopefully it stays dead and isn't brought back in 2028. Also remember, this is hot on the heels of The Supreme Court ruling Mail in Ballots are valid, legal, and counted. https://www.rawstory.com/maga-berserk-scotus-ruling/ --UPDATE-- This is before the report that it's dead, but it looks like they might be trying to slip it into the National Defense Bill - https://thehill.com/homenews/house/5946645-speaker-johnson-save-america-ndaa/
item.dc:creator: Imadethisaccounttopost

item.title: GovBrief Today #519
item.description: Three weeks ago I started showing you government getting it right, and I wish I'd started sooner. The Good Government category debuted at issue #500, and your comments keep saying the same thing: thank you for the hope that good government still exists. Since then I've run 19 of these stories: Oregon teamed with Dolly Parton to mail every child under five a free book each month. New Jersey is cleaning up group homes and funneling the fines back into better care. Texas rebuilt the state's websites for residents with disabilities. Two rural Tennessee counties made the data-center industry wait until the rules were written. The president opened the country's 250th birthday by calling American support for NATO "ridiculous," and nobody is pretending the national picture is fine. But politics still influences your life most where you live. Keep your focus on your community, because local change is the kind that grows, town to state to nation. I've emailed 8,082 stories over 519 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/ Today's full brief: https://go.govbrief.today/260703-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-nato-ridiculous [2] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-july3 [3] https://go.govbrief.today/iowa-snap [4] https://go.govbrief.today/pollution-pardons [5] https://go.govbrief.today/doj-fuel-probe [6] https://go.govbrief.today/murrill-warrant [7] https://go.govbrief.today/ice-camp-neglect [8] https://go.govbrief.today/fair-heat [9] https://go.govbrief.today/nj-group-homes Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos

item.title: Do the woke celebrate July 4th?

item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered

item.description: Today celebrates the signing of the Declaration of Independence. It lists the acts of tyranny that justify the Declaration. I consider reading them a minimum requirement of this day. The paragraph immediately after them is also particularly powerful. Anyone who does not stand by them by definition cannot be a patriot or claim to be proud to be American. https://www.archives.gov/founding-docs/declaration-transcript
item.dc:creator: ChewyTheWookie

item.title: We're not gonna make it, are we.....people I mean?
item.dc:creator: CrimsonPermanentAssurance

item.title: Revolution Calling
item.description: (Midterm voting starts on July 3rd....) It's July 4th. It's early in the morning, and I'm exhausted. My neighbors decided they wanted to set off fireworks for most of last night. I know I'm not the only one affected by this. There are people with young kids, animals, and other disabled veterans who are under an incredible amount of stress right now, much like I am. My physical conditions have flared up, including seizures and stomach issues that I don't need to go into. And it's not because of the jump scares. It's largely because of the overwhelming feeling of dread and the awareness of the continued erosion of our constitutional rights. I'm depressed because it feels like too few people are standing up against the tyranny that's on display. So many flag-flying Americans swore up and down that they would resist a tyrannical government. Now many openly support it, pouring money into a billionaire who couldn't care less whether they live or die. The stress is made even worse knowing it's the beginning of the month, the energy bills keep climbing, and I'm losing the ability to move forward with anything—let alone accomplish the basics. I feel like my legs have been cut out from under me by this administration, just like so many other Americans who are paying attention. My anger has long since boiled away. Everything has evaporated as I watch people claim the title of "patriot" despite never having done anything selfless for their communities, let alone their country. And you don't have to serve in the military to earn that title. You just have to be you—a citizen, or someone striving to become one—going through your daily life and choosing to be better than the example our current administration sets. That's what it takes to be a patriot. That's what it takes to be an American: helping your neighbor, no matter who they are. As far as I'm concerned, any celebration should wait until after the midterm elections—if enough of you show up and begin to set this country back on a better course. http://youtube.com/post/UgkxrYngCyeDW3ASydVUCJaTEjySy9K1DMyB
item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered
